study.name: NCT01797120
study.description:
Post-menopausal women with hormone-receptor positive (HR+) metastatic breast cancer resistant to aromatase inhibitor (AI) therapy will be randomized to receive Fulvestrant (Faslodex) with Everolimus or Fulvestrant (Faslodex) with a placebo (no active ingredients).

Fulvestrant has demonstrated activity when used as first, second, or third line endocrine therapy, making it an attractive therapy for combination with other agents. In addition, it is commonly reserved for use following disease progression on AI therapy.

Everolimus is an orally administered drug that blocks a signaling pathway called "mTOR". "mTOR" acts as a regulator for many processes in the body, including cell growth. Blocking this pathway may have an effect on cell growth.

The combination of a novel class of agents (mTOR inhibitors) and an established standard treatment for metastatic HR+ breast cancer may potentially increase the clinical benefit by targeting multiple different biological pathways.
study.label: Phase II - Hormone-receptor positive (HR+) metastatic breast cancer resistant to aromatase inhibitor (AI)

studyDesigns.name: Study of Fulvestrant +/​- Everolimus in Post-Menopausal, Hormone-Receptor + Metastatic Breast Ca Resistant to AI (PrE0102)
studyDesigns.label: Phase II - Hormone-receptor positive (HR+) metastatic breast cancer resistant to aromatase inhibitor (AI)
studyDesigns.description:
Post-menopausal women with hormone-receptor positive (HR+) metastatic breast cancer resistant to aromatase inhibitor (AI) therapy will be randomized to receive Fulvestrant (Faslodex) with Everolimus or Fulvestrant (Faslodex) with a placebo (no active ingredients).

Fulvestrant has demonstrated activity when used as first, second, or third line endocrine therapy, making it an attractive therapy for combination with other agents. In addition, it is commonly reserved for use following disease progression on AI therapy.

Everolimus is an orally administered drug that blocks a signaling pathway called "mTOR". "mTOR" acts as a regulator for many processes in the body, including cell growth. Blocking this pathway may have an effect on cell growth.

The combination of a novel class of agents (mTOR inhibitors) and an established standard treatment for metastatic HR+ breast cancer may potentially increase the clinical benefit by targeting multiple different biological pathways.

estimands.name: Estimand for Progression-Free Survival (PFS)
estimands.label: Primary Estimand
estimands.description: Comparison of PFS between fulvestrant plus everolimus versus fulvestrant plus placebo in AI-resistant HR+ metastatic breast cancer. Constructed retrospectively per ICH E9(R1); this protocol predates the 2019 addendum.
estimands.populationSummary: Post-menopausal women with HR+ (ER/PR+, HER2-negative) stage IV or inoperable locally advanced breast cancer that is resistant to aromatase inhibitor therapy; ECOG PS 0-1; no prior mTOR inhibitor; up to one prior systemic chemotherapy for metastatic disease. Randomized 1:1; approximately 120 eligible patients.

intercurrentEvents.name: Death before documented progression
intercurrentEvents.label: Death before documented progression
intercurrentEvents.text: Patient dies from any cause before radiological disease progression has been documented per RECIST v1.1.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Composite: death is included as a PFS event (composite endpoint definition per §6.1.6). The time-to-event variable captures whichever occurs first: progression or death.

intercurrentEvents.name: Discontinuation of everolimus due to toxicity
intercurrentEvents.label: Discontinuation of everolimus due to toxicity
intercurrentEvents.text: Patient permanently discontinues everolimus or placebo due to an adverse event but continues on fulvestrant monotherapy.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Treatment-policy: patient remains on study and continues fulvestrant alone per §5.5. Follow-up for progression and survival continues. PFS events occurring after everolimus discontinuation are included in the primary analysis regardless of treatment status.

intercurrentEvents.name: Withdrawal of consent or loss to follow-up
intercurrentEvents.label: Withdrawal of consent or loss to follow-up
intercurrentEvents.text: Patient withdraws consent or is lost to follow-up before a PFS event is documented.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Hypothetical (censoring): patient is censored at the date of last tumor assessment. The estimand implicitly assumes non-informative censoring — that censored patients would have experienced events at the same rate as those who remained in follow-up.

intercurrentEvents.name: Initiation of prohibited anti-cancer therapy before progression
intercurrentEvents.label: Initiation of prohibited anti-cancer therapy before progression
intercurrentEvents.text: Patient receives a prohibited anti-cancer agent before disease progression is documented.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Treatment-policy: protocol prohibits new anti-cancer agents before progression (§5.3.4). If such a violation occurred, the patient would remain in the analysis with events counted as observed. No censoring at time of prohibited therapy initiation is specified.

estimands.name: Estimand for Overall Survival (OS)
estimands.label: Secondary Estimand — OS
estimands.description: Comparison of overall survival between treatment arms. Descriptive; no pre-specified alpha or power.
estimands.populationSummary: All randomized eligible patients (same as PFS estimand population).

intercurrentEvents.name: Discontinuation of study treatment (any reason)
intercurrentEvents.label: Discontinuation of study treatment (any reason)
intercurrentEvents.text: Patient permanently discontinues all study treatment due to disease progression, toxicity, or other reasons.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Treatment-policy: all patients who discontinue treatment are followed for survival per §5.5. Deaths after treatment cessation are counted as OS events regardless of reason for discontinuation.

intercurrentEvents.name: Initiation of subsequent anti-cancer therapy after progression
intercurrentEvents.label: Initiation of subsequent anti-cancer therapy after progression
intercurrentEvents.text: Patient initiates a new anti-cancer regimen after disease progression on study treatment.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Treatment-policy: OS reflects real-world outcome including the effect of any subsequent therapies. No censoring or adjustment for post-progression treatment is specified.

intercurrentEvents.name: Withdrawal of consent or loss to follow-up
intercurrentEvents.label: Withdrawal of consent or loss to follow-up
intercurrentEvents.text: Patient withdraws consent or cannot be contacted for survival follow-up.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Hypothetical (censoring): patient is censored at date of last known alive contact. Non-informative censoring assumed.

estimands.name: Estimand for Time to Progression (TTP)
estimands.label: Secondary Estimand — TTP
estimands.description: Comparison of time to disease progression, excluding deaths as events. Descriptive.
estimands.populationSummary: All randomized eligible patients (same as PFS estimand population).

intercurrentEvents.name: Death before documented progression
intercurrentEvents.label: Death before documented progression
intercurrentEvents.text: Patient dies from any cause before radiological disease progression has been documented per RECIST v1.1.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Hypothetical (censoring): death without prior progression results in censoring at time of death. This reflects the hypothetical question 'what would the time to progression have been if the patient had not died?' — the key mechanistic distinction from the PFS estimand.

intercurrentEvents.name: Discontinuation of study treatment
intercurrentEvents.label: Discontinuation of study treatment (any reason)
intercurrentEvents.text: Patient permanently discontinues study treatment before disease progression.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Treatment-policy: follow-up for progression continues after treatment discontinuation per §5.5. TTP events occurring after treatment cessation are included.

estimands.name: Estimand for Objective Response Rate (ORR)
estimands.label: Secondary Estimand — ORR
estimands.description: Between-arm comparison of the proportion of patients achieving confirmed CR or PR in patients with measurable disease. Descriptive.
estimands.populationSummary: Subset of randomized eligible patients with at least one measurable lesion at baseline per RECIST v1.1.

intercurrentEvents.name: Death or progression before first response assessment
intercurrentEvents.label: Death or progression before first response assessment
intercurrentEvents.text: Patient dies or has documented progression before achieving CR or PR at any post-baseline tumor assessment.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Composite: patients who die or progress without ever achieving a confirmed CR or PR are counted as non-responders in the ORR denominator.

intercurrentEvents.name: Treatment discontinuation before response assessment
intercurrentEvents.label: Treatment discontinuation before response assessment
intercurrentEvents.text: Patient discontinues all study treatment before a post-baseline tumor assessment confirming CR or PR.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Treatment-policy: patients who discontinue without a prior confirmed response are counted as non-responders. No censoring at time of treatment discontinuation.

estimands.name: Estimand for Clinical Benefit Rate (CBR)
estimands.label: Secondary Estimand — CBR
estimands.description: Between-arm comparison of the proportion of patients with CR, PR, or SD maintained for at least 6 months. Descriptive.
estimands.populationSummary: All randomized eligible patients including those with measurable and non-measurable disease.

intercurrentEvents.name: Death or progression before 6-month stable disease threshold
intercurrentEvents.label: Death or progression before 6-month stable disease threshold
intercurrentEvents.text: Patient dies or has documented progression before 6 months of confirmed stable disease, partial response, or complete response.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Composite: patients who do not achieve and maintain CR, PR, or SD for at least 6 months are counted as not deriving clinical benefit, regardless of cause.

intercurrentEvents.name: Treatment discontinuation before 6-month landmark
intercurrentEvents.label: Treatment discontinuation before 6-month landmark
intercurrentEvents.text: Patient discontinues study treatment before the 6-month landmark required for SD to qualify as clinical benefit.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Treatment-policy: patients who discontinue and lack tumor assessment confirming ≥6 months of SD are counted as non-responders for CBR.

estimands.name: Estimand for Safety and Tolerability
estimands.label: Secondary Estimand — Safety
estimands.description: Descriptive characterization of the incidence and severity of adverse events in the safety-evaluable population. No formal hypothesis test.
estimands.populationSummary: All patients who received at least one dose of any study treatment. Approximately 65 patients per arm.

intercurrentEvents.name: Treatment discontinuation
intercurrentEvents.label: Treatment discontinuation
intercurrentEvents.text: Patient permanently discontinues study treatment. AE collection continues for 30 days after last dose of any study drug per §5.5.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: While-on-treatment: TEAEs are collected only during the treatment period and the 30-day post-treatment follow-up window. Adverse events occurring beyond this window are not captured as TEAEs in the primary safety analysis.

intercurrentEvents.name: Dose reduction or interruption of everolimus
intercurrentEvents.label: Dose reduction or interruption of everolimus
intercurrentEvents.text: Patient undergoes dose modification (reduction or temporary interruption) of everolimus or placebo per protocol dose-modification rules.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Treatment-policy: AEs that prompt dose modification are recorded regardless of the modification. Events are attributed to the randomized treatment arm and included in the safety summary as observed.

indications.name: Hormone Receptor-Positive Metastatic Breast Cancer Resistant to Aromatase Inhibitor Therapy
indications.label: HR+ AI-Resistant Metastatic Breast Cancer
indications.description: Post-menopausal women with hormone-receptor positive (ER/PR+, HER2-negative) stage IV or inoperable locally advanced breast cancer that is resistant to aromatase inhibitor therapy, defined as relapse while receiving adjuvant AI or progressive disease while receiving an AI for metastatic disease.

objectives.name: Primary Objective
objectives.label: Primary Objective
objectives.description: To assess progression-free survival in post-menopausal patients with hormone-receptor positive metastatic breast cancer that is resistant to aromatase inhibitor therapy treated with fulvestrant and everolimus compared to fulvestrant alone.

endpoints.name: Progression-Free Survival (PFS)
endpoints.label: Progression-Free Survival (PFS)
endpoints.description: Time from randomization to documented disease progression or death, whichever occurs first. Subjects who have neither progressed nor died are censored at the date of last tumor assessment. Disease progression assessed per RECIST v1.1.
endpoints.purpose: To demonstrate that the addition of everolimus to fulvestrant improves PFS compared to fulvestrant plus placebo (hypothesized median PFS improvement from 5.4 to 9.2 months; HR ≈ 0.59).

objectives.name: Secondary Objective
objectives.label: Secondary Objective
objectives.description: To describe the safety profile, objective response rate, time to progression, and overall survival in this patient population.

endpoints.name: Overall Survival (OS)
endpoints.label: Overall Survival (OS)
endpoints.description: Time from randomization until death from any cause. Subjects alive at the time of analysis are censored at the date of last follow-up.
endpoints.purpose: To characterize overall survival in the two treatment arms.

endpoints.name: Time to Progression (TTP)
endpoints.label: Time to Progression (TTP)
endpoints.description: Time from randomization until objective disease progression per RECIST v1.1. Unlike PFS, deaths without prior progression are not counted as events (subjects are censored at time of death).
endpoints.purpose: To estimate and compare median time to disease progression between treatment arms.

endpoints.name: Objective Response Rate (ORR)
endpoints.label: Objective Response Rate (ORR)
endpoints.description: Proportion of patients with a confirmed complete response (CR) or partial response (PR) per RECIST v1.1. Assessed in patients with measurable disease at baseline.
endpoints.purpose: To compare the proportion of patients achieving an objective tumor response between treatment arms.

endpoints.name: Clinical Benefit Rate (CBR)
endpoints.label: Clinical Benefit Rate (CBR)
endpoints.description: Proportion of patients with stable disease (SD) for ≥6 months, partial response (PR), or complete response (CR) — i.e., CR + PR + SD ≥ 6 months — per RECIST v1.1.
endpoints.purpose: To compare the proportion of patients deriving clinical benefit (response or prolonged stable disease) between treatment arms.

endpoints.name: Safety and Tolerability
endpoints.label: Safety and Tolerability
endpoints.description: Incidence, severity (CTCAE v4.0 grade), and type of adverse events, including overall toxicity, grade 3–4 toxicities, serious adverse events (SAEs), and second primary cancers. Assessed in all patients who received at least one dose of study treatment.
endpoints.purpose: To characterize and compare the safety profile of fulvestrant plus everolimus versus fulvestrant plus placebo.

scheduleTimelines.label: Main Timeline
scheduleTimelines.description: The main timeline for the study

instances.name: CPHASE

studyInterventions.name: Fulvestrant + Everolimus (Arm A)
studyInterventions.label: Fulvestrant + Everolimus (Arm A)
studyInterventions.description: Fulvestrant 500 mg IM on Day 1 of each 28-day cycle (Day 1 and 15 of Cycle 1 only) plus Everolimus 10 mg orally once daily.

studyInterventions.name: Fulvestrant + Placebo (Arm B)
studyInterventions.label: Fulvestrant + Placebo (Arm B)
studyInterventions.description: Fulvestrant 500 mg IM on Day 1 of each 28-day cycle (Day 1 and 15 of Cycle 1 only) plus matching oral placebo once daily.

bcSurrogates.name: Magnesium Measurement
bcSurrogates.label: Magnesium Measurement (C64840)
bcSurrogates.description: A quantitative measurement of the amount of magnesium present in a sample.

bcSurrogates.name: Hepatitis B DNA Measurement
bcSurrogates.label: Hepatitis B DNA Measurement (C103404)
bcSurrogates.description: A measurement of the Hepatitis B virus DNA in a biological specimen

bcSurrogates.name: Hepatitis B Surface Antigen
bcSurrogates.label: Hepatitis B Virus Surface Antigen Measurement (C64850)
bcSurrogates.description: A measurement of the surface antigen reaction of a biological specimen to the Hepatitis B virus.

bcSurrogates.name: HBsAb+
bcSurrogates.label: Hepatitis B Surface Antibody Positive (C153243)
bcSurrogates.description: An indication that antibodies that recognize a hepatitis B virus surface protein have been detected in a sample.

bcSurrogates.name: HBsAb-
bcSurrogates.label: Hepatitis B Surface Antibody Negative (C153244)
bcSurrogates.description: An indication that antibodies that recognize a hepatitis B virus surface protein have not been detected in a sample.

bcSurrogates.name: HCV RNA Positive
bcSurrogates.label: HCV RNA Positive (C162042)
bcSurrogates.description: An indication that hepatitis C RNA has been detected in a sample.

bcSurrogates.name: PFTs with DLCO
bcSurrogates.label: Pulmonary function tests (PFTs) that include DLCO (Diffusing Capacity of the Lungs for Carbon Monoxide) (C38081)
bcSurrogates.description: A broad range of tests that are performed to assess how well lungs inhale and exhale air and how efficiently they transfer oxygen into the blood.

bcSurrogates.name: Dietary Phosphorus Measurement
bcSurrogates.label: Dietary Phosphorus Measurement (C184481)
bcSurrogates.description: A determination of the phosphorus in a nutritional product or meal, or a portion thereof.

bcSurrogates.name: Adherence
bcSurrogates.label: Adherence (C25729)
bcSurrogates.description: The act of abiding by a stated treatment plan or protocol.

bcSurrogates.name: Patient Compliance
bcSurrogates.label: Patient Compliance (C39490)
bcSurrogates.description: The extent to which a patient conforms to medical advice regarding prescribed regimen, lifestyle, dietary changes, follow-up schedule and other recommendations.

bcSurrogates.name: Compliance Monitoring
bcSurrogates.label: Compliance Monitoring (C62737)
bcSurrogates.description: The act of continuously assessing whether an individual is adhering to their treatment plan.

bcSurrogates.name: Non-Compliance With Study Drug
bcSurrogates.label: Non-Compliance With Study Drug (C49631)
bcSurrogates.description: An indication that a subject has not agreed with or followed the instructions related to the study medication. (NCI)

bcSurrogates.name: Non-Compliance with Study Product
bcSurrogates.label: Non-Compliance with Study Product (C210354)
bcSurrogates.description: An indication that a subject has not agreed with or followed the instructions related to the study product.

bcSurrogates.name: Trial Compliance
bcSurrogates.label: Trial Compliance (C142734)
bcSurrogates.description: Adherence to clinical trial-related requirements, good clinical practice (GCP) requirements, and the applicable regulatory requirements. (ICH)
